task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace GUI is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A 15-sided closed polygon resembling a V-block is drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch is visible in the workspace. It must have a flat bottom, two inward rectangular slots on the left and right sides, and a V-shaped notch on the top edge, forming a single closed loop.

task_completion.description: Overall width and height dimensions are applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines are visible on the sketch showing '60.0' for the total width and '40.0' for the total height.
Dimension lines are visible showing '30.0' for the width of the top V-notch opening and '90' (or similar) for the internal angle of the V-notch.
Dimension lines are visible showing '5.0' for the depth of the side slots, '10.0' for their height, and '15.0' for the distance from the bottom edge to the bottom of the slots.
The SolveSpace property browser (usually top left) displays '0 degrees of freedom' or indicates the sketch is fully constrained (e.g., 'ok').

task_completion.description: The design is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The window title bar displays 'v_block_profile.slvs' or a save dialog shows this filename being saved in the Documents/SolveSpace directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the sketch.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace interface with tools being selected and the sketch being actively drawn, rather than terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write the .slvs file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) is seen editing a .slvs file directly.
Multiple screenshots show the sketch evolving from a few lines to a fully constrained polygon, rather than appearing instantly in its final form.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a 15-sided closed polygon in SolveSpace representing a V-block. Expected dimensions: overall width 60.0 mm, overall height 40.0 mm. The side slots must be 5.0 mm deep, 10.0 mm high, and start 15.0 mm from the bottom. The top V-notch must be 30.0 mm wide with a 90-degree internal angle. The sketch must be fully constrained (0 degrees of freedom) and saved as '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/v_block_profile.slvs'.
